author: gaxanh
content:
In fact, even SELFDESTRUCT is an exception, although it is still more useful than re-delegation in many cases, for example, creating temporary execution containers without maintaining the account long-term
In my opinion, if SELFDESTRUCT had removed the functions that burdened Verkle, it would be a more worthwhile refund target than re-delegation

author: garyrong
content:
<@1361003005691822160> You are right!

> creating temporary execution containers without maintaining the account long-term
It's possible to pay for the account-creation while at the end, the account is gone with zero state growth. Specifically:
- Selfdestruct contract A, B as the beneficiary with non-zero balance
- Deploy contract on B with create2
- Selfdestruct B
We charge the account-creation fee at step (1), but no refill at step (3). Thanks for catching it up

author: garyrong
content: <@830368514225340436> OK, as Ga Xanh pointed out, re-delegation is not the only exception and my point is meaningless. I am fine for simplicity preference
